item.title: Musk’s SpaceX raises $75bn in world’s biggest IPO
item.description: Rockets-to-AI group priced its shares at $135 in deal that drew blockbuster investor demand

item.title: Corporate climate plans arbiter draws critics on new ‘net zero’ rule book
item.description: Companies able to claim they have made ‘best efforts’ even if they fall short of climate goals

item.title: The AI public market floodgates are opening
item.description: The huge sums that Wall Street is about to be asked for look like being only a down payment

item.title: ‘Toxic’ social media overwhelms police response to Belfast violence
item.description: Fallout from knife attack and riots accelerated by online posting, current and former officers say

item.title: New York Knicks’ winning streak should rub off on its owner too
item.description: Shares in Madison Square Garden Sports, which also owns hockey team the New York Rangers, have doubled in a year

item.title: SpaceX IPO marks the end of the no-brainer benchmark index trade
item.description: Investors do not need big benchmarks to get what they want

item.title: Time to axe ‘unfair’ pensions triple lock, says UK’s cost of living tsar
item.description: Iceland chair Lord Richard Walker, appointed by Starmer in February, calls system ‘mathematically unsustainable’

item.title: The ‘new joule order’ is here. The west is last to realise
item.description: Electrification is the purchase of optionality and China bought more of it than any nation in history

item.title: It’s not easy when you’re big in Seattle
item.description: Yet another proxy oddity

item.title: The unlikely alliance pushing an AI sovereign wealth fund
item.description: Even some of the tech labs seem to agree that society as a whole should benefit from advances

item.title: Well actually, SpaceX is a UK growth stock
item.description: POP goes a weasel

item.title: Jeff Bezos says AI will bring ‘golden ages’ not mass job losses
item.description: Amazon founder lays out vision for new $41bn AI lab Prometheus

item.title: India protests to US after three sailors killed in tanker attack
item.description: New Delhi calls for ‘de-escalation’ in Gulf but appears to have little appetite for confrontation with Washington

item.title: World Bank warns El Niño risks driving up global food prices
item.description: Weather phenomenon threatens to worsen problems for farmers dealing with high fertiliser costs due to Iran war

item.title: Russia ‘not looking for conflict’, says Nato’s top US commander
item.description: General Grynkewich’s remarks come as Washington withdraws key assets from Europe

item.title: Arms maker Diehl in talks to build Ukrainian missile in Germany
item.description: Groups to discuss whether they could jointly produce Fire Point’s Flamingo cruise missile

item.title: FT readers tell us how AI is changing their jobs
item.description: A special postbag edition

item.title: AI-generated film about Iran protests turns tragedy into slop
item.description: The first such movie accepted by a festival, ‘Dreams of Violets’ portrays victims of state brutality without capturing their humanity

item.title: Why opposition to battery storage projects is growing across the US
item.description: Resistance is rising as the technology becomes more essential to meet surging power demand

item.title: FirstFT: Iran threatens Hormuz after renewed US strikes
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Brokers prepare for SpaceX test and prediction markets take $2bn bets ahead of World Cup

item.title: Environmental reporting non-profit group CDP strikes private equity deal
item.description: Permira to take majority stake in core operations under energy transition strategy

item.title: Artificial chocolate will show what really shapes global trade
item.description: Lab-grown cocoa is the latest synthetic commodity to challenge a natural product

item.title: ‘Anything could happen’: SpaceX IPO poses big test for Wall Street banks
item.description: Elon Musk’s rockets-to-AI group is listing at a fixed price during a bout of volatility in US markets

item.title: Beijing online shopping warning hits shares in Chinese tech groups
item.description: Alibaba and JD.com admonished by regulator for misleading sales tactics during ‘6.18’ event

item.title: After the final whistle: media groups battle for World Cup attention
item.description: YouTube, podcasts and live events are opening new ways to monetise the tournament

item.title: Applied Materials in Singapore and Japan’s new subsea cable approach
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Mike Ashley’s Frasers offers to buy Hugo Boss in €2.7bn deal
item.description: Retail billionaire’s company launches takeover bid for German fashion group

item.title: MFS auditors investigated by UK accountancy regulator
item.description: FRC probes firms that signed off accounts across collapsed UK mortgage lender’s network

item.title: CMA investigates Ryanair over fees for parents sitting with their children
item.description: UK competition regulator looking into whether policy breaches consumer law

item.title: Libor 2.0?
item.description: The death of London’s infamous interest rate benchmark has left a hole
